title: Latent Embedding Adaptation for Human Preference Alignment in Diffusion Planners

stages: Pretraining a diffusion model without reward supervision using a large state-action-only sequence dataset
Adapting the model to specific preferences using a small set of human labels
Generating trajectories aligned with the learned preferences
challenges_addressed: Computational efficiency for edge device deployment
Preventing model deviation from the base model

related_approaches.name: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)
related_approaches.description: Learns reward models to capture individual preferences then refines policies
related_approaches.limitation: Computationally expensive due to many parameters

related_approaches.name: DPO (Direct Policy Optimization)
related_approaches.description: Directly aligns policies with human preferences without a separate reward model
related_approaches.limitation: Computationally expensive and requires careful tuning

results.claim: Adapts more accurately to human preferences with less data
results.validation: Tested on both offline datasets and custom dataset with real human labels

figure.caption: Overview of personalizing decision-making models. We leverage large-scale offline data for pretraining, followed by rapid and efficient personalization using small-scale preference data.

contributions: Introducing a reward-free pretraining approach that jointly learns meaningful representations for PLE
